claims.claim: NVIDIA disclosed its 800 VDC architecture for AI factories via a developer blog dated 20 May 2025, timed to Computex 2025.
Full-scale 800 VDC production is targeted to coincide with NVIDIA Kyber rack-scale systems hosting 576 Rubin Ultra GPUs in 2027.
800 VDC supports racks ranging from 100 kW to over 1 MW on the same datacenter power infrastructure.
NVIDIA claims 800 VDC delivers up to 5% end-to-end power-efficiency improvement vs current 54 V systems.
NVIDIA states 800 VDC reduces copper requirements by 45% at row level relative to the 54 VDC baseline.
Holding 54 VDC distribution in-rack would require up to ~200 kg of copper busbar per 1 MW rack and consume up to 64 U of rack space at MW scale.
A 1 MW rack at 54 VDC draws roughly 18,500 A; at 800 VDC the same rack draws roughly 1,250 A, a 14.8x current reduction.
Because I^2R losses scale as the square of current, the 14.8x current drop yields approximately a 219x reduction in conductor resistive losses for equivalent power.

The OCP Diablo 400 (internal codename Mt. Diablo) specification, co-authored by Google, Meta, and Microsoft, defines a sidecar power rack supporting +/-400 VDC bipolar or 800 VDC monopolar topologies for IT racks from 100 kW to 1 MW.
Google states it chose 400 VDC as the nominal voltage specifically to leverage the supply chain established by electric vehicles for economies of scale and proven quality.
Vertiv plans to release its 800 VDC power portfolio in H2 2026, aligned to support the 2027 rollout of NVIDIA Rubin Ultra platforms.
Schneider Electric introduced an 800 VDC Sidecar capable of powering racks up to 1.2 MW, with modular conversion shelves and modular energy storage.
Eaton unveiled an 800 VDC reference architecture at OCP Global Summit 2025 using supercapacitor ride-through storage, an ORV3-compatible busbar, and an 800 VDC sidecar prototype.

ABB acquired a minority stake in DG Matrix in March 2025; DG Matrix's Power Router uses a solid-state transformer to convert HVAC input to 800 VDC and is claimed to be up to 5x smaller with up to 98% efficiency.

Delta Electronics disclosed a 72 kW AC-DC server power shelf (400-480 Vac to 800 Vdc) at up to 98% efficiency and a 90 kW DC-DC shelf (800 Vdc to 50 Vdc) at up to 98.5% efficiency, plus a 1.1 MW In-Row Power product yielding ~92% end-to-end efficiency.
Texas Instruments unveiled a complete 800 VDC power architecture with NVIDIA on 16 March 2026 covering 800 V down to GPU core voltage, including a bus converter with 97.6% peak efficiency.
On the 21 April 2026 Vicor Q1 earnings call, CEO Patrizio Vinciarelli called the initiative to convert directly from 800 V to 6 V 'ill-conceived and internally inconsistent.'
Vertiv stock closed at $128.41 in June 2025 and $323.91 in May 2026, a 152% 12-month gain that step-changed +28% in October 2025 around the NVIDIA collaboration and Q3 earnings call.

Vicor stock returned approximately 634% in the 12 months to late May 2026 despite the CEO's public rejection of the 800-to-6 V topology and the company's absence from NVIDIA's named 800 VDC silicon partner list.
Vertiv trades at roughly 48x forward earnings versus Eaton at ~32x and Schneider Electric at ~24x, reflecting AI-driven growth premium.
The Porsche Taycan launched in production on 9 September 2019 as the first production vehicle with an 800 V system voltage.
Hyundai's E-GMP 800 V platform debuted in the Ioniq 5 in early 2021, bringing 800 V architecture to mainstream price segments and supporting up to 350 kW DC fast charging.
China 800 V passenger car sales reached 840k units in 2024, equal to 6.9% penetration, with 9.5% projected for 2025 and >70 800 V models on sale by mid-2025.

Wolfspeed FY2024 design-ins ran roughly 75% automotive with ~70% tied to 800 V platforms, indicating that the SiC fab capacity now redirected toward AI datacenters was bootstrapped by the EV industry.
On the BG2 podcast released in November 2025, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the biggest issue is no longer a compute glut but power, and that warm shells to plug chips into are the constraint.
Amazon CEO Andy Jassy on the Q2 2025 earnings call called power AWS' single biggest constraint, expected to take several quarters to resolve.
Microsoft Q1 FY2026 capex hit $34.9B and Microsoft guided FY2026 capex to roughly $190B, ~61% YoY growth, while remaining capacity-constrained through at least year-end.
The OpenAI-Oracle-Crusoe Stargate Abilene project comprises eight AI factory halls totaling 1.2 GW and 500,000 NVIDIA GPUs, the first node in a planned ~7 GW Stargate buildout.
PJM Interconnection reported that AI infrastructure projects entering service in 2025 took an average of more than seven years to reach operational status.
ERCOT's large-load interconnection queue reached approximately 226 GW in November 2025, nearly 4x the 63 GW at end-2024, with ~77% from data centers.
Dominion Energy reported about 40 GW of data center capacity in various stages of contracting as of December 2024, up from ~21 GW in July 2024.
Constellation Energy signed a 20-year, 835 MW PPA with Microsoft in September 2024 to restart Three Mile Island Unit 1, backed by a $1B DOE loan with restart targeted for 2028.

After FERC rejected Talen's expanded behind-the-meter arrangement in November 2024, Talen restructured into a 17-year, $18B, 1,920 MW front-of-meter PPA with AWS from Susquehanna nuclear in June 2025.
GE Vernova's gas-turbine slot-reservation backlog reached ~80 GW by end-2025, with the CEO expecting turbine reservations sold out through 2030 by end-2026.
OSHA fixes the energized threshold at 50 V AC or DC, and NFPA 70E specifies DC approach boundaries from 100 V to 800 kV — placing an 800 VDC rack firmly in qualified-electrical-worker territory.
Under IEC 61140 / 60038, the low-voltage band caps at ≤1,500 V DC, so 800 VDC stays in the LV regime; NEC 2026 reorganizes around the same 1,500 V DC threshold via new Article 270.
Vacuum interrupter DC rating is typically only 15-30% of its AC rating because DC arcs lack the periodic zero crossings that quench AC arcs, driving the shift to hybrid solid-state breakers.

ISO 6469-3 Voltage Class B (>60 V DC) mandates isolation monitoring, HV interlock loops, equipotential bonding, and automatic discharge of DC-link capacitors to <60 V within 5 s — practices the datacenter HVDC industry now imports from EVs.
Meta's Catalina ORv3 high-power rack supports up to 140 kW and remains 48 V; Meta has demonstrated an ORv3-HPR V4 rack at ±400 V (~800 V equivalent) scaling to ~800 kW at OCP EMEA 2025.
Direct-to-chip liquid cooling is mandatory above ~175 kW per rack; air tops out near 20 kW, rear-door HX near 100 kW.
NVIDIA designed Vera Rubin NVL72 for 45 °C facility-water inlet, eliminating mechanical chillers and contributing to what Jensen Huang framed as ~6% of global data-center power saved.
Ecolab agreed to acquire CoolIT Systems for $4.75B in March 2026 at roughly 8.6x forward revenue, evidence of hyperscaler capex pulling liquid-cooling suppliers up the multiples ladder.
BNEF projects AI data-center copper demand averaging ~400,000 t/yr over the next decade, peaking at 572,000 t in 2028 with 4.3 Mt cumulatively locked into datacenters by 2035.
COMEX copper futures (HG=F) traded near $4.33/lb in July 2025 and ~$6.47/lb in May 2026, a 49% rise in ten months that sharpens the strategic case for the 45% on-rack copper savings.

Solid-state-transformer commercial deployment timelines from non-NVIDIA vendors run late 2026 demo → 2027 pilot → 2028 volume per Enphase's IQ SST roadmap, raising slip risk versus NVIDIA's 2027 Rubin Ultra ramp.
Conventional power transformer lead times have stretched to roughly four years, and ~95 large-load interconnection requests in PJM through November 2025 totaled ~54 GW.

NVIDIA's October 2025 800 VDC partner ecosystem includes ABB, Eaton, GE Vernova, Heron Power, Hitachi Energy, Mitsubishi Electric, Schneider Electric, Siemens, and Vertiv on datacenter power systems, plus 10+ silicon vendors including TI and Infineon.

Google has deployed liquid cooling at GigaWatt scale across more than 2,000 TPU pods over the past seven years at consistently about 99.999% uptime, anchoring the ±400 VDC plus liquid-cooling Mt. Diablo plan.
Three workers sustained burns from an arc-flash incident at Google's Council Bluffs facility in 2022, illustrating the pre-800 V baseline of datacenter electrical injury.
Brookfield committed $5B in October 2025 to deploy up to 1 GW of Bloom Energy solid-oxide fuel cells behind-the-meter at AI data centers, with the first 55 MW project online Q4 2025.
